title: viburnum villosum var. subdentatum
intro: Viburnum villosum var. subdentatum, also known as the Chinese arrowwood, is a deciduous shrub native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its dense, thorny branches and clusters of white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Viburnum villosum var. subdentatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant activity, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or as a dried extract, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
